summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1987 Long Beach show featuring a smoking Morning Dew, The Other One and an epic Watchtower.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear soundboard quality with superb instrument separation, making every nuance audible. Fans rave about the electric second set – highlighted by a blistering Morning Dew, a soaring The Other One, and what many call the best Watchtower of the year. Overall the performance feels infused with electricity and camaraderie, delivering an unforgettable Dead experience.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (10‑plus) give 4–5 stars and specifically commend both the musical performance and the high‑quality soundboard source; only a few note an average show, keeping the overall rating strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with excellent separation of instruments; reviewers repeatedly describe the sound as "great", "super quality" and note no major glitches.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A lively first set followed by an electrified second set that builds to a powerful Watchtower; the pacing feels natural and the band stays tightly locked throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as "maniac" on guitar with ferocious energy; his playing is highlighted during The Other One and Watchtower.
band_member_comments.Bob: Called "possessed" on vocals/guitar, especially on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums>Space segment noted for giving a "contact buzz" – a thrilling rhythmic moment.
